Do not edit this file directly. -->  # ARC-QUIC-CRT-0016 - QUIC Server-Role Post-Proof Peer Transport-Parameter Commit Gate Architecture  ## Purpose  Describe the narrow server-role post-proof gate that lets staged peer transport parameters become committed only after the existing inbound client Finished proof exists, while keeping transcript milestones and broader handshake-completion claims separate.  ## Requirements Satisfied  - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0103 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0118 - REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S8-0002  ## Design Summary  The connection library keeps the server-role peer transport-parameter commit gate as a follow-on decision above the existing inbound client Finished proof slice rather than folding commit into transcript progression or broader completion semantics. The existing transcript-progress owner remains responsible for ordered Handshake CRYPTO assembly, deterministic message framing checks, and staging peer transport parameters from ClientHello. The existing managed key-schedule owner remains responsible for deriving the expected client Finished verify data and publishing PeerFinishedVerified only after cryptographic verification succeeds against the complete local server flight. Above those existing states, the bridge keeps commit explicit: it admits a PeerTransportParametersCommitted update for the server role only when the bridge is non-terminal, the completed Finished boundary still describes the same TLS session, staged peer transport parameters still exist, PeerFinishedVerified already exists, and the commit candidate exactly matches the staged peer transport parameters. PeerHandshakeTranscriptCompleted remains a separate transcript milestone rather than the commit gate, and the runtime continues to consume only explicit bridge updates without gaining 1-RTT readiness, client identity claims, or interop support.  ## Key Components  - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransport.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTransportTlsBridgeState.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransportBridgeDriver.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0117.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0118.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S8-0002.cs  ## Risks  - The gate must stay role-aware so client-role commit semantics do not regress. - PeerHandshakeTranscriptCompleted must remain a transcript milestone and must not become an implicit commit gate or a broader completion claim. - PeerFinishedVerified must remain a narrow TLS proof and must not be widened into client identity, client-certificate authentication, trust, hostname semantics, or interop readiness. - The bridge and runtime must reject repeated, conflicting, fatal-state, and mismatched commit attempts deterministically rather than committing by construction.  ## Related Code And Tests  - [`src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs`](../../../src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs) -
